So let's get right into it. The first part of how to set up your business for under $1,000 is having a great idea. Having great ideas is very, very critical and some people get married to their idea and they should because your business is almost like your spouse. Something that you're going to be doing for one year, five years, 10 years or the rest of your life. So you want to really love it and you want it to be something that's important to you. A lot of times people get involved in a business that they don't care about and it truly hurts them in the long run because they start to resent it. Similar to getting into a bad relationship. If you marry somebody just to marry them, well after a couple years or five or 10 years you start to resent them and you just build up and that's why there's such a large divorce rate. There's also a large business failure rate because a lot of the times people are just resentful of how much work goes into it and not reaping the rewards. However, if you have a great idea and you love what you do, you're willing to make sacrifices, you're willing to put in much more hours and you're willing to talk about things with much more exasperation and much more excitement because you love it. Same way if you're in a great relationship, you're just more excited to talk about it, do it and be a part of it. So having a great idea, there are steps to that. One, you do a lot of research. You need to Google like crazy, you need to research everything you can and figure out who your competitors are, not just your direct competitors, but also around that space. So let's say you want to do a yoga studio. You want to understand everything about a yoga studio, yoga classes, yoga online seminars, do people have a juice bar inside their yoga stations, do people have how much equipment they need, you have to do a lot to find out is your idea something that matters and do people care? Some people might say come up with something in the plumbing industry or in the kitchen space and it might be a great idea but there might be massive competition and multi-billion dollar companies that you may not want to fight against. However, you might come up with the idea for a yoga studio and you're in a territory that barely has it, you want to figure out does your idea matter in Ohio or Wichita, Kansas? Do people care about doing yoga? You also want to make sure that your idea fits the area that you're in. If you're selling online, you just want to make sure that it fits the right price point if you're going for the high-end, mid-tier or extremely affordable low-end pricing. So having a great idea, the first thing you want to do is talk to your friends, talk to your family. Tell them about your idea and don't be scared that people are going to go run off and steal your idea. A lot of times entrepreneurs are very worried and they want to sign NDA agreements. The reality is the faster you start and go out and build your great company, it doesn't matter because if you have any success, I promise you're going to get knocked off. When Fubu clothing was out there doing $400 million in retail sales, they were doing over $500 to $600 million in fake counterfeit sales about their brand. So once you come out with something and it does well, like a hoverboard came out like we did with U-Wheels, all of a sudden there's 40, 50, 60, 70 brands because everybody wanted to mimic something they saw doing very well. So don't get caught up in the fact that you have a great idea and you don't want to share it and you hold it too close to your vest because then nothing is going to happen. So talk to your friends, talk to your family members, talk to business advisors, get referrals. So if your friend knows a guy that owns a chain of restaurants and you want to get into the restaurant space or your friend knows somebody that owns a yoga studio, ask them for advice. Go to their classes, go to the local conventions, go to local seminars, look at eventbrite.com, look at meetup.com. There's a lot of great events to find out a lot about the space that you want to be in. So talk, ask, find out. There's so much now that you can research for free. You're not spending a penny to just find out if your idea is great.
